task.name: Tone Extraction
task.description: Extract voice signature from text across emotional tone, structural patterns, and vocabulary dimensions
task.objective: Extract voice signature from text by analyzing emotional tone, structural patterns, and vocabulary/register. Output is written IN the extracted voice, creating a meta-demonstration that serves as a direct writing guide.

llm.rule: Read the complete task before starting execution.
NEVER write third-person analysis — "The writer uses..." is FORBIDDEN.
NEVER list features — demonstrate voice through structure and vocabulary, not description.
Write the voice profile IN the extracted voice — you ARE the voice.
Match source language exactly — Portuguese input requires Portuguese output.

substep.title: Read Analysis Framework
substep.action: Internalize all three dimensions: emotional tone, structural patterns, vocabulary/register.

substep.title: Get Input
substep.ask: Provide the text for voice analysis (paste directly, file path, or URL).

substep.title: Load Text
substep.action: If file path or URL provided, load the content.

substep.title: Emotional Tone
substep.action: Identify vulnerability level, self-reflection depth, questioning intensity.
Note emotional honesty (raw vs. filtered) and perspective stance.

substep.title: Structural Patterns
substep.action: Document list/prose ratio, nesting depth, sentence length patterns.
Note question density, bold/emphasis usage, paragraph structure.

substep.title: Vocabulary and Register
substep.action: Capture formality spectrum, domain-specific vocabulary, language mixing.
Note metaphor usage, hedge words, first-person intensity.

substep.title: Identify Signature
substep.action: Identify 2-3 signature elements that make this voice immediately recognizable.

substep.title: Write In-Voice
substep.action: Create 3-5 paragraph profile (400-600 words) written IN the extracted voice.
Paragraph 1: Open with emotional core — establish psychological register.
Paragraph 2: Demonstrate structural preferences through format.
Paragraph 3: Showcase vocabulary patterns — domain terms, formality, mixing.
Optional paragraphs: Address tone variations, close with meta-reflection.

substep.title: Validate Output
substep.action: Verify: Written IN voice, not ABOUT voice. No meta-commentary.
Verify: All three dimensions represented. Source language matched.
Verify: Formatting preferences mirrored. Usable as direct writing guide.

halt-conditions.condition: Source text not provided — request text before proceeding.
Text too short (under 100 words) — request longer sample for accurate extraction.
